title: age-group — canonical per-sport cutoff samples
description: Reference material — canonical cutoff patterns per sport, sourced from public federation documentation. NOT loaded at runtime — this file exists as a copy-paste starter for tenants creating sport-specific age groups (age_group_sport_specific entitlement, Enterprise-only). The tenant's actual overrides are stored as regular AgeGroup rows with the appropriate sport_key + cutoff_date_kind + cutoff_month + cutoff_day fields set.
notes: Federation rules change. This file is a starter, not a source of truth — tenants must confirm their federation's current cutoff with the governing body before shipping to production.
The `cutoff_date_kind='custom_date'` samples use cutoff_month + cutoff_day; academic_year samples use only cutoff_month (day defaults to 1); calendar_year and rolling_from_birthday use neither.
This module does NOT ship a runtime sport-registry — sport_key values in this file are informative. Wave 3c will introduce a registered sports catalogue + tighten valid_sport_key validation.

football_european.description: European football (soccer) youth categories cutoff at 1 January. An athlete's competitive age for a season = year_of_season - year_of_birth. UEFA + most national FAs follow this.

football_american_us_soccer.description: US Soccer aligned with the 1 August calendar cutoff in 2015. Some state associations still use 1 January; check locally before shipping.

basketball_fiba.description: FIBA uses calendar year (1 January) for its international youth categories.

swimming_fina.description: Swimming uses age-on-day-of-event (rolling from birthday). An athlete's competitive category is decided by their actual age on the first day of competition — a swimmer who turns 13 during a meet remains in the 12-and-under age group for the whole meet.

tennis_itf.description: ITF junior categories cut at 1 January.

rugby_world_rugby.description: World Rugby age-grade cuts at 1 January. Some national unions use 1 September for school-year alignment (see rugby_england_school).

cricket_icc.description: ICC age-grade cuts at 1 September (aligned with the northern-hemisphere school year, which is when most cricket-playing nations schedule their youth calendars).

athletics_wa.description: World Athletics youth + junior categories use calendar year (1 January). Masters use rolling_from_birthday (rolling from birthday) for competitive divisions above 35.

gymnastics_fig.description: FIG uses calendar year. Elite-development ranges are narrow: junior (13-15) precedes senior (16+); women's artistic gymnastics has been 16+ for senior competition since 1997 (raised from 15).

school_program.description: Schools + academic programmes typically align age groups with the academic year (September cutoff). Athletes born September onward are 'one year older' for programme purposes.
